question:
Will CR Flamengo win on 2025-10-22?

Description: In the upcoming game, scheduled for October 22, 2025
If CR Flamengo wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

Resolution source: https://conmebollibertadores.com/

evidences.title: Ranking the Eight Favorites to Win the 2025 Copa Libertadores
evidences.content: Flamengo have been the best team in all of South America in 2025, losing just five of their last 50 games across all competitions. Filipe Luis’s team are a well-oiled machine, with European-caliber players all over the XI, including new signings Emerson Royal, Samuel Lino and Saúl Ñíguez. El Mengão currently top of the table in the Brazilian league and have become synonymous with continental success in recent times, winning two Copa Libertadores in the last 10 years. Giorgian de Arrascaeta has five goals in his last five appearances and starting striker Pedro has five in his last six. Aside from Palmeiras, Flamengo are simply a cut above the rest in the tournament. evidences.title: Copa Libertadores records and statistics
evidences.content: The Copa Libertadores has seen its matches distributed primarily across South American countries with strong football traditions, reflecting the tournament's structure where home teams host group stage and early knockout matches, while later stages often feature two-legged ties or neutral venues. Brazil leads in hosting final matches (legs of two-legged finals plus single finals), with a total of 36, attributed to the frequent qualification of its clubs like Palmeiras, Flamengo, and Grêmio to the decisive stages. Argentina follows closely with 35 final matches hosted (including the 2024 single final at Estadio Más Monumental in Buenos Aires), driven by powerhouses such as Boca Juniors and River Plate. Uruguay ranks third with 21 finals, all at the Estadio Centenario in Montevideo, [...] (#ref-3)](#ref-3) This table highlights Argentine clubs' historical edge, with River Plate and Boca Juniors ranking in the top four and collectively accounting for 10 of Argentina's 25 titles—tied with Brazil for the most by any nation. Nacional holds the record for most participations with 433 matches, underscoring Uruguayan persistence despite fewer titles, while River Plate leads in total wins...

evidences.title: Flamengo v Racing Club: Copa Libertadores 2025 Semi-Final Preview
evidences.content: Racing Club are chasing their first Copa Libertadores title since 1967. The current Copa Sudamericana holders have been in impressive form this season and, much like Flamengo, have played an exciting brand of football. They will now look to take their momentum one step further as they prepare for this high-stakes clash against the Brazilian giants. To put their progress into perspective, since Gustavo Costas began his third spell in charge on December 15, 2023, Racing Club have played 102 matches, winning 58, drawing 13 and losing 31. During this period, they have scored 174 goals and conceded 90, giving Costas a win rate of 56.86%. [...] By Louis Smith. The first of the 2025 Copa Libertadores semi-finals delivers a classic Brazil vs Argentina showdown, as Flamengo face Racing Club in a matchup that rarely disappoints. The two sides have met four times since 2020, with three of those encounters ending in draws within normal time. Flamengo claimed their only victory during the 2023 group stage, winning 2–1. However, their meeting in the 2020 Round of 16 is one Flamengo fans won’t forget. After two tightly contested 1–1 draws, the tie went to penalties, where Racing held their ner...

evidences.title: Flamengo v Racing Club: Copa Libertadores 2025 Semi-Final Preview
evidences.content: Since securing their place in the Copa Libertadores semi-finals, Flamengo’s form has been somewhat inconsistent but largely positive. They have won three matches, drawn one, and lost once during that run. Their most recent performance came at the weekend, where they delivered a strong display to move level on points with Palmeiras in the race for the Brasileirão title, edging their rivals 3–2 in a thrilling encounter. That victory will serve as a major confidence boost heading into this clash against a Racing Club side who will be eager to take down another Brazilian outfit. [...] In the Quarters, they faced Estudiantes, where Flamengo took control with a 2–0 lead after goals from Pedro and Guillermo Varela inside the opening 10 minutes. Rubro-Negro seemed firmly in charge of the tie. However, despite what looked like a relatively comfortable night for Flamengo, they allowed Estudiantes to claw their way back into the match through a Leo Pereira own goal in the dying embers of the first leg. [...] Racing Club are chasing their first Copa Libertadores title since 1967. The current Copa Sudamericana holders have been in impressive form this season and, much like Flamengo, have pla...

evidences.title: Flamengo-Palmeiras final bringing Brazil to standstill
evidences.content: ### A decade of domestic dominanceA decade of domestic dominance The rise of the _Mengão_ and the _Verdão_ in the Libertadores reflects their dominance in the Brazilian top flight – they have won six of the last nine Campeonato Brasileiro Série A titles between them. Palmeiras lifted the trophy in 2016, 2018, 2022 and 2023, while Flamengo were champions in 2019 and 2020. During that period, Palmeiras were runners-up in 2017 and 2024, and Flamengo in 2018 and 2021. This year’s title race is going down to the wire once again, with the two giants both in the running: Palmeiras currently lead the way on 65 points, with Flamengo hot on their heels and just a point behind. It promises to be a fight to the bitter end, with every match now being treated like a cup final.

evidences.title: Flamengo v Racing Club: Copa Libertadores 2025 Semi-Final Preview
evidences.content: By Louis Smith. The first of the 2025 Copa Libertadores semi-finals delivers a classic Brazil vs Argentina showdown, as Flamengo face Racing Club in a matchup that rarely disappoints. The two sides have met four times since 2020, with three of those encounters ending in draws within normal time. Flamengo claimed their only victory during the 2023 group stage, winning 2–1. However, their meeting in the 2020 Round of 16 is one Flamengo fans won’t forget. After two tightly contested 1–1 draws, the tie went to penalties, where Racing held their nerve, converting every spot-kick to eliminate Flamengo. [...] In 2025, they have been Brazilian slayers, which began in emphatic fashion with a 4–0 aggregate victory over Botafogo in the Recopa Sudamericana, a result that gave them plenty of confidence heading into the start of the Libertadores. They carried that momentum into domestic action, defeating Fortaleza 3–0 at home before securing a 1–0 win in the home match to secure top spot in the group. In the Quarters, Racing Club’s most recent Copa Libertadores outing came in the quarter-finals against fellow Argentine side Vélez Sarsfield. In the first leg, an Adrián Martínez goal secured a...

evidences.title: ‘Simplified’ yellow card suspension rule to be introduced for 2025/26 season in Ligue 1 - Yahoo Sports
evidences.content: The decision comes as a result of the LFP’s disciplinary working group, established earlier this year by the league’s Board of Directors in conjunction with Ligue 1 and Ligue 2 representatives. Inspired by practices across other major European leagues, the new system is designed to simplify the accumulation process and improve clarity for players, clubs, and fans alike. The updated rule – officially adopted by the LFP on the 3rd June – will apply across all domestic competitions, including the Coupe de France and the Trophée des Champions. As before, all card counts will be reset at the end of each season. [...] Soccer image Soccer image ... evidences.title: Brazilian Team to Practice on Campus Before FIFA Club World Cup - News | Stockton University
evidences.content: Facts about CR Flamengo Will arrive at Stockton June 11 to practice before first two FIFA Club World Cup 2025 games. Games are in Philadelphia on June 16 (Esperance Sportive de Tunisie) and June 20 (Chelsea FC). CR Flamengo is the most popular team in Brazil with more than 46.9 million fans. Founded in 1895 and named after the Flamengo neighborhood of Rio de Janeiro. Rubro-Negro (Scarlet and Black) is a popular nickname. | | [...] ## Brazilian Team to Practice on Campus Before FIFA Club World Cup February 6, 2025 stockton athletics complex Stockton University will host one Brazil's most iconic and globally beloved soccer team's, CR Flamengo, as the team prepares to compete in the FIFA Club World Cup 2025 this summer. Galloway, N.J. — One of Brazil’s most iconic and globally beloved soccer teams has announced that it will train at Stockton University this summer, ahead of its first two matches for the FIFA Club World Cup 2025™. CR Flamengo, based in Brazil’s largest city Rio de Janeiro, has an estimated fan base of nearly 50 million and is reportedly the country’s most valuable soccer team.
